data.title: IGV Software ETF crashes 24% in worst quarter since '08, Quant rates top holdings
data.description:
The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF ($IGV) cratered more than 24% in Q1 2026, its worst quarterly plunge since Q4 2008, erasing gains and dragging software names into bear territory amid AI disruption fears and growth slowdowns, FactSet data shows.

Below is a list of the top 10 holdings of the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) ranked according to their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. The list highlights stocks within the technology and software sectors based on their quantitative performance metrics.

Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) leads the list with the highest Quant Rating of 3.47 among the group. Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Oracle Corporation (ORCL) follow closely behind with ratings of 3.42 and 3.39, respectively.

All top holdings currently carry “Hold” ratings according to the Quant system, with scores ranging from 3.47 at the top down to 2.79 at the bottom of the list. This indicates a neutral outlook across the leading software sector holdings.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades stocks based on their relative performance on critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the list:

  1. Microsoft Corporation (MSFT), Quant Rating: 3.47

  2. Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR), Quant Rating: 3.42

  3. Oracle Corporation (ORCL), Quant Rating: 3.39

  4. AppLovin Corporation (APP), Quant Rating: 3.26

  5. ServiceNow, Inc. (NOW), Quant Rating: 3.19

  6. Salesforce, Inc. (CRM), Quant Rating: 3.12

  7. CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (CRWD), Quant Rating: 3.06

  8. Adobe Inc. (ADBE), Quant Rating: 3.06

  9. Intuit Inc. (INTU), Quant Rating: 2.80

  10. Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW), Quant Rating: 2.79

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Chevron confirms exclusive power supply deal with Microsoft, Engine No. 1
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) confirmed Thursday it entered into an exclusivity agreement with Microsoft (MSFT) and investment fund Engine No. 1 for the negotiation of a proposed power generation and electricity offtake arrangement, although no commercial terms have been finalized, and no definitive agreement has been reached so far.

Bloomberg had reported Microsoft's (MSFT) involvement in a proposed natural gas-fired power plant in West Texas with a projected cost of ~$7B that would initially generate 2,500 MW of electricity to power ​a large ⁠data center campus.

Microsoft (MSFT) is aggressively building data centers as it battles for supremacy in artificial intelligence, increasing its AI capacity by 80% last year and planning to double its data center footprint over the next two years, and access to reliable baseload power is a key challenge—one that the Chevron (CVX) and Engine No. 1 partnership expects to address given its extensive natural gas production in West Texas and contracts for large turbines.

"While we are in exclusive negotiations, and subject to the execution of definitive agreements and certain conditions being satisfactorily met, the parties do not intend to comment further," Chevron (CVX) said in a statement.

data.title: XLRE holds up better than S&P 500; EQIX, IRM, DLR lead gains
data.description:
The Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLRE) posted a slight increase of 1.11% in FY26 Q1, while the broader S&P 500 Index fell ~4.6%, which shows REITs performed relatively better than the overall equities ongoing crash.

However, the housing market is still weak. Existing home sales are still ~25% lower than 2019 levels and are at their weakest since 1995. Small drops in mortgage rates below 6% led to short-term refinancing activity, but rates have climbed again due to geopolitical tensions. 

This weak housing trend could drag the U.S. economy down and hurt consumer sentiment as 2026 moves forward, said Bret Jensen, a market analyst and investing group leader at Seeking Alpha.

Mortgage rates reportedly increased last week. The 30-year rate rose to 6.38% from 6.22%, but it is still lower than 6.65% a year ago. Similarly, the 15-year rate also rose to 5.75% from 5.54%, slightly lower than last year.

On the positive side, according to Sam Khater of Freddie Mac, the housing market is slowly improving. More people are applying for home loans compared to last year, and rates are still better on an annual basis.

Amid the ongoing market pressure, investors are again focusing on premium real estate assets, as per Jonathan Goldstein, co-founder and CEO of Cain International. He remained positive about long-term opportunities in the Middle East, expecting strong growth in the Gulf region over the next 10-15 years.

Top 5 Performers (Q1 2026):

  • Equinix (EQIX) +25.83%
  • Iron Mountain (IRM) +17.66%
  • Digital Realty Trust (DLR) +13.23%
  • Kimco Realty (KIM) +10.11%
  • Regency Centers (REG) +8.49%

Bottom 5 Performers (Q1 2026):

  • CoStar Group (CSGP) -39.20%
  • Boston Properties (BXP) -23.21%
  • CBRE Group (CBRE) -17.20%
  • Mid-America Apartment Communities (MAA) -12.37%
  • SBA Communications (SBAC) -12.20%

data.title: Heinz edges lower after a six session rally
data.description:
Shares of The Kraft Heinz Company (KHC) slipped 0.84% to close at $22.26 on Wednesday, snapping a six session rally.

The stock has gained around 5.5% between March 24 and March 31 compared to a 0.43% decline in the S&P 500 during the same period.

The food and beverage company climbed amid a period when consumer staples were drawing attention for oversold signals. Today’s modest decline marks a short-term pullback, in line with broader defensive positioning in the consumer staples sector amid market volatility linked to U.S.-Iran tensions.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, KHC is rated a Hold, with a score of 3.06 out of 5, receiving an A for valuation but an F in terms of growth.

An analyst said Heinz may appear attractive due to its low valuation and high dividend yield, but underlying fundamentals remain weak, adding that persistent declines in revenue and margins alongside negative total returns signal deeper structural issues, and pointing out, “Low valuation ratios likely reflect dismal market sentiment rather than compelling value.”

On Wall Street, one of 20 analysts rates the stock with a strong buy, 15 recommend a hold, and four of them rate it with a sell or lower.

Shares have fallen around 8.4% in the past month and remain down about 8.1% year-to-date.

data.title: Recession odds down sharply after string of better-than-expected economic data
data.description:
Recession odds on prediction market Kalshi have plummeted 26.6% in just one week, with investors betting the probability of an economic downturn down 26% on Wednesday alone, following a string of better-than-expected economic data.

However, despite the recent optimism, recession chances remain elevated at 26.6% compared to a month ago, reflecting ongoing uncertainty about the broader economic trajectory.

 

 

The U.S. manufacturing sector showed continued resilience in March, with the ISM Manufacturing PMI expanding to 52.7, exceeding both the 52.3 consensus forecast and the prior reading of 52.4, according to data from the Institute for Supply Management.

The New Orders Index also expanded for the third consecutive month, marking 17 straight months of overall economic expansion.

In addition, consumer spending proved robust in February, with retail sales climbing 0.6% month-over-month to $738.4 billion, according to advance data from the U.S. Census Bureau—well above the 0.4% consensus and reversing January’s 0.1% decline.

Core retail sales, excluding motor vehicles and parts, jumped 0.5% against expectations of just 0.3%.

David Russell, global head of market strategy at TradeStation, attributed the strength partly to tax refunds, stating, “The consumer was healthy going into the oil spike. It’s a positive for GDP, suggesting rate cuts aren’t needed.”

Moreover, private sector employment exceeded expectations in March, growing by 62,000 jobs compared to the consensus forecast of 40,000, according to ADP’s National Employment Report. The education and health services sector led the gains, adding 58,000 positions.

More on the U.S. economy

  • Shifts in the money supply are making it harder for the Fed to cut rates – Jeremy Siegel
  • Goldman Sachs: Interest rate hikes are ‘much less likely’ to happen
  • Business inventories unexpectedly slip M/M in January
  • ISM Manufacturing PMI unexpectedly rises in March
  • PMI Manufacturing index for March rises marginally less than initially estimated

data.title: Tilray reaffirms FY2026 adjusted EBITDA of $62M-$72M as BrewDog deal targets $1.2B annualized revenue run rate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Tilray Brands (TLRY) Q3 FY2026

Management View

  • CEO Irwin Simon framed the quarter around M&A-driven scale and diversification, saying, "We delivered a record quarter with continued international expansion across our platforms" (President, CEO & Chairman Irwin Simon), and highlighting BrewDog: "This transaction positions Tilray at approximately $1.2 billion global revenue company on an annualized basis" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Simon emphasized Q3 operating momentum and liquidity, citing net revenue of $207 million, gross profit of $55 million, and "$265 million in cash, restricted cash and marketable securities" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon), while also calling out international cannabis as "the largest quarter ever for international cannabis growth" with $24.1 million in net sales and "73% year-over-year growth" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • On international and distribution strategy, Simon said Tilray Pharma grew "35% year-over-year to $83 million" and pointed to partnerships expanding German reach: "expanding our reach to more than 16,000 pharmacies, up from 13,000 previously" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon), alongside a U.K. distribution partnership with Smartway (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • On beverages, Simon said Tilray executed "a strategic licensing partnership with Carlsberg" starting January 2027 and "the targeted acquisition of BrewDog" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon). He described BrewDog as creating "approximately $500 million global craft beverage platform on a pro forma basis" and said Tilray acquired it for "approximately EUR 40 million" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • CFO Carl Merton tied results to record revenue and reiterated guidance, stating, "Net revenue was a third quarter record of $206.7 million" and "we are reaffirming our fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62 million to $72 million" (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Carl Merton).

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed profitability guidance: "we are reaffirming our fiscal 2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62 million to $72 million" (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Tilray described incremental growth vectors that were not in Q2’s prepared remarks, including the Carlsberg U.S. partnership beginning "in January of 2027" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon) and expected impact: "We expect this to drive immediate scale accretive to revenues" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • On hemp-derived THC beverages, Simon outlined three regulatory scenarios for HDD9 after November 2026: "it gets extended and stays as is," a new regulated-milligram framework, or "the ban in November of 2026 happened, and it completely stops" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

Financial Results

  • Tilray reported Q3 net revenue of $206.7 million (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton), with segment net revenue of $64.8 million cannabis, $83 million distribution, $42.6 million beverage, and $16.4 million wellness (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Profitability metrics included gross profit of $55 million and gross margin of 27% (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton). By segment, Tilray reported cannabis gross margin of 40%, distribution gross margin of 12%, beverage gross margin of 32%, and wellness gross margin of 33% (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Earnings and cash flow items included net loss of $25.2 million and net loss per share of $0.24 (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton), plus adjusted net income of $2.4 million and adjusted net income per share of $0.02 (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton). Tilray reported adjusted EBITDA of $10.7 million and cash flow used in operations of $21.9 million, with $264.8 million in cash, restricted cash and marketable securities and a net cash position of $3.5 million (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

Q&A

  • Kaumil Gajrawala, Jefferies: asked how Tilray balances supporting international cannabis growth with Canada stabilizing; President, CEO & Chairman Simon cited supply expansion and yield improvements: "we are bringing on our Masson grow facility... we're going from 137 metric tonnes of grow to almost 200 metric tonnes" and said Europe’s need is "consistent supply" while acknowledging price compression (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Kaumil Gajrawala, Jefferies: asked whether Project 420 is followed by a new productivity program; President, CEO & Chairman Simon said, "there is absolutely project ongoing" and pointed to additional savings potential from combining procurement and operations as Tilray’s beer platform grows "almost $0.5 billion now in size" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Xin Ma, TD Cowen (Victor Ma): asked what drove international growth and Germany’s 43% growth; President, CEO & Chairman Simon said permits timing "equals out" and that growth reflected "supply and demand," adding, "I'm not ready to give that yet" on a run-rate (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Xin Ma, TD Cowen (Victor Ma): asked about aluminum exposure and Carlsberg scale benefits; CFO Merton said, "we're currently hedging 65% to 75% of our buy on a month-to-month basis, and we're hedging a year out" (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Aaron Grey, Alliance Global Partners: asked about HDD9 regulation and CMS pilot opportunity; President, CEO & Chairman Simon said Tilray is prepared with Happy Flower drinks and is working through "the right approvals" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Pablo Zuanic, Zuanic & Associates: pressed on Germany vertical integration, route-to-market, and pharmacy reach; Rajnish Ohri said Tilray has "a team of about 20-plus people" working with prescribers and "about 7 to 8 people" supporting pharmacies via telecall services (Rajnish Ohri), while President, CEO & Chairman Simon argued CC Pharma’s "16,000 pharmacies" and medical licensing support future category expansion (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was slightly negative to neutral on beverages and regulatory uncertainty, pressing on margin volatility, shelf resets, and hemp-derived THC risk, including questions on aluminum costs and whether beverage margins were at a “trough” (Xin Ma, TD Cowen; Kenric Tyghe, Canaccord Genuity).

  • Management tone was slightly positive in prepared remarks and more defensive in Q&A on beverages and price compression, including CFO Merton stating, "we think this represents the bottom" for beverage margins (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton) and Simon repeatedly emphasizing scale and supply advantages in Europe (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Versus Q2, management commentary shifted from U.S. cannabis rescheduling emphasis to M&A and beverage-platform scaling, with BrewDog and Carlsberg dominating the Q3 narrative (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 reiterated the same adjusted EBITDA guidance range as Q2: $62 million to $72 million (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Q3 introduced major strategic changes not present in Q2: the BrewDog acquisition (EUR 40 million) and the Carlsberg U.S. licensing partnership beginning January 2027 (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Q3 highlighted a shift in mix and growth emphasis toward international cannabis and distribution execution, including explicit discussion of reallocating supply to higher-margin international markets and calling international growth "more indicative of what investor expectations should be going forward" (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Analyst focus in Q3 broadened toward procurement hedging, beverage margin trajectory, and Germany route-to-market execution, whereas Q2 questions leaned more into hemp-derived THC regulatory outcomes and U.S. medical strategy framing.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing price pressure internationally, with CFO Merton saying price compression "reduced international cannabis revenue by approximately $7 million" (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton) and Simon stating Tilray "overcame $7 million in price pressure that flows directly to the bottom line" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

  • Beverage headwinds included overhead underutilization and input costs; CFO Merton said cost savings were "largely offset" by "almost $2.9 million of higher aluminum costs year-to-date and lower overhead utilization rates" (CFO & Principal Accounting Officer Merton).

  • Regulatory uncertainty remained a key theme in U.S. hemp-derived THC beverages, with Simon flagging potential changes "after November 2026" (President, CEO & Chairman Simon).

Final Takeaway

Tilray’s management positioned Q3 FY2026 around record net revenue of $206.7 million, an accelerating international cannabis and European distribution engine, and a reshaped beverage strategy centered on Project 420 completion, a January 2027 Carlsberg U.S. partnership, and the acquisition of BrewDog for approximately EUR 40 million. The company reaffirmed FY2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62 million to $72 million, while management and analysts focused heavily in Q&A on sustaining international momentum amid price compression, stabilizing beverage margins under higher aluminum costs and utilization challenges, and navigating U.S. hemp-derived THC regulatory outcomes by November 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Grail partnering with Superpower to offer Galleri cancer early detection test
data.description:
• Grail (GRAL) and preventative health platform Superpower are partnering for the latter to provide the former's Galleri multi-cancer early detection test.
  • The test can detect a signal found in more than 50 cancer types. It can also predict a cancer's signal origin.
  • Grail's PATHFINDER-2 study found that adding Galleri to recommended cancer screenings boosted cancer detection more than seven-fold.

data.title: Nucor, Commercial Metals rated Buy at Goldman in bullish view of U.S. steel stocks
data.description:
Nucor (NUE) and Commercial Metals (CMC) both rise 2.7% in Wednesday's trading as Goldman Sachs started coverage with Buy ratings and respective $210 and $74 price targets, expecting U.S. steel shipments to rise as a result of declining imports due to tariffs, as U.S. producers increase volumes to compensate for the lack of international steel imports.

The bank said it expects U.S. steel shipment growth will outpace demand growth in 2026, while volume growth for domestic shipments is still greater than import volume growth in 2027 before moderating in 2028.

The current steel price environment should act as a tailwind for all producers with metal price exposure, but given the recent pullback as a result of inflation and global growth fears, Goldman's Nick Cash and Cecilia Tang see current prices providing an attractive entry point for select U.S. steel equities, particularly Nucor (NUE) and Commercial Metals (CMC).

Goldman's Buy rating on Nucor (NUE) is supported by three key factors: the company is poised to outperform the U.S. steel market through rising end-market demand, import share gains, and growth-related volume expansion via its West Virginia mill; structural cost advantages that should drive margin expansion; and a transition from heavy capital investment to a free cash flow harvesting phase.

Cash and Tang said Commercial Metals (CMC) is a Buy because of strong North America end-market demand with exposure to U.S. regions with above-average growth; inorganic and organic margin expansion and growth position the company well ahead of the cycle; and a strategic focus on prioritizing balance sheet health and cash flow generation over immediate capital returns.

The analysts took a Neutral view on Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), citing caution on the high fixed cost structure of the business as steel prices likely have posted near-term highs.

data.title: Iran’s Pezeshkian tells Americans to ‘look beyond political rhetoric’
data.description:
Iranian leader Masoud Pezeshkian released an open letter Wednesday urging Americans to “look beyond political rhetoric and reconsider the realities of Iran’s past, present, and aspirations for a future defined not by confrontation, but by truth, dignity, and mutual understanding.”

In the message, Pezeshkian argued that the perceived Iranian threat is a fabrication of the military-industrial complex and Israeli political interests, describing it as a “manufactured narrative” designed to justify military dominance and sustain the arms industry.

The Iranian leader emphasized that his country “has never, in modern history, chosen the path of aggression, expansion, colonialism, or domination, and never initiated any war.”

Pezeshkian characterized recent Iranian military actions as “a measured response grounded in legitimate self-defense,” pointing to the concentration of American military bases surrounding his country as evidence of the threat Iran faces.

Pezeshkian questioned whether the U.S. government is truly putting “America first” or acting as a “proxy for Israel” willing to fight “to the last American soldier.”

He accused Israel of manufacturing the Iranian threat to divert global attention from its actions against Palestinians while shifting the financial and human costs onto American taxpayers and the broader region.

Pezeshkian concluded the letter saying that “continuing along the path of confrontation is more costly and futile than ever before,” inviting Americans to speak with those who have visited Iran rather than relying on the “machinery of misinformation.”

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

More on war in Iran

  • Iran: Future of Hormuz will be decided between Iran and Oman; Trump's ceasefire claims 'false and baseless'
  • Pentagon expands missile supply chain with plan to triple Patriot seeker output
  • U.S. will consider Iran ceasefire once Hormuz opens, Trump says
  • Countdown to Trump’s address to the nation: What will he say?
  • Iran threatens attacks on Nvidia, Apple and other tech majors: report

data.title: Sam's Club raises its annual membership fees - matches BJ's, still lower than Costco
data.description:
Sam's Club announced on Wednesday that it will raise its annual membership fee by $10 to $60 for basic membership and $120 for its higher-tier option. The price hike will be the first from the retailer since it raised annual fees in October 2022.  The new price will go into effect on May 1.

In a statement, Sam’s Club noted that it adjusted membership pricing to support the things our members love, pointing to the wide assortment, expanded hours, and better curbside pickup and delivery options.

The new fee structure is still below the current rates that Costco (COST) charges but matches the annual rates currently charged by BJ's Wholesale Club (BJ).

Notably, Sam's Club recorded its highest membership count ever at the end of its last reporting period, although the Walmart (WMT)-owned business did not release an exact figure. Sam's Club posted a 4.0% increase in comparable sales in Q4, as a 5.3% jump in transactions offset a 1.3% decline in average ticket. Adjusted operating income was up 3.8% during the quarter, which did not match the pace from the Walmart U.S. and Walmart International segments.
